stoevdesigngroup.brief: SDG can claim the intersection where environmental responsibility meets structural design intelligence—reframing sustainability from marketing language into evidence-based spatial practice. The convergence of material authenticity trends with botanical precision creates an opening to establish plants-as-architecture as the sophisticated alternative to decorative landscaping. The market's shift toward material honesty and quiet luxury creates space for SDG to differentiate environmental stewardship through observed evidence rather than proclaimed values. This moment allows the practice to establish structural planting as the sophisticated alternative to both decorative landscaping and sustainability theater, positioning design intelligence as the bridge between site response and environmental responsibility.

d-outdoor-kitchen-and-living-systems.opinion: We design outdoor living environments where cooking becomes part of the site's experience, not an imported indoor function. When we position screening plants to create wind protection around a cooking

d-deep-blue-green-cabinetry.why: Interior trend with no outdoor space relevance or design insight opportunity

d-kitchen-islands-and-work-zones.why: Interior trend with no outdoor space relevance or design insight opportunity

d-scalloped-and-wave-tile.why: Falls outside SDG's expertise in experience design and structural planting; positions firm as interior finisher rather than experience designer

d-turquoise-color-schemes.why: Opportunity to reframe color choice as site response rather than aesthetic preference, but requires specific project context
d-turquoise-color-schemes.opinion: We respond to what the site offers before we introduce color. Turquoise emerges naturally where light reflects off water onto limestone, where salt air meets indigenous succulents. The color becomes i

d-architectural-presence-and-scale.why: Direct alignment with core conviction about plants as architecture and experience design philosophy
d-architectural-presence-and-scale.opinion: We use plants as architecture to create presence and scale. A single mature Quercus agrifolia positioned to frame a view carries more architectural weight than any hardscape element. The scale emerges

d-complete-seclusion-and-privacy.why: Opportunity to reframe privacy from defensive barrier to experiential intimacy using plants as architecture
d-complete-seclusion-and-privacy.opinion: We create privacy through strategic placement rather than defensive barriers. A grove of Cupressus sempervirens positioned to frame morning light provides seclusion while maintaining connection to the

d-art-collection-display.why: Direct alignment with art-informed design conviction and experience design philosophy, leveraging SDG's art world expertise
d-art-collection-display.opinion: We approach art integration as spatial dialogue, not display case installation. A James Turrell piece requires different site relationships than a Richard Serra sculpture. The landscape responds to th

d-window-maintenance.why: Falls into forbidden instructional/maintenance category and positions firm as trade contractor rather than experience designer
